summary.one_paragraph: Retry logic is useful but mixed with HTTP handling in one type, lacks concurrency tests, and the CL description omits idempotency requirements. Address blockers before merge.

overall_code_health.rationale: Improves retries but increases coupling in OrderProcessor.

cl_description.rationale: Title is clear; body does not mention idempotency or worker pool impact.

change_scope.rationale: Mostly one feature but includes unrelated logging refactor.

design.rationale: Processor owns HTTP client, retry, and persistence.

functionality.rationale: Race possible when two workers retry the same order id.

complexity.rationale: process() exceeds 120 lines with nested error branches.

tests.rationale: Happy path only; no concurrent retry or exhaustion cases.

naming.rationale: Names are clear despite large type.

comments.rationale: Sparse on why idempotency is required.

documentation.rationale: README not updated for new retry semantics.

context_and_review_depth.rationale: Logging change in same file obscures retry diff.

good_things: Exponential backoff parameters are centralized in RetryPolicy.

findings.observation: OrderProcessor constructs reqwest::Client, runs retry loops, and calls SqlStore in one type.
findings.why_it_matters: Mixed responsibilities make failure modes hard to reason about and violate ordering boundary in docs/adr/0001-ordering-boundaries.md.
findings.recommended_action: Extract HttpClient and RetryExecutor; keep OrderProcessor as orchestration only.
findings.evidence: struct OrderProcessor { client: Client, store: SqlStore, policy: RetryPolicy }

findings.observation: Two concurrent `process(order_id)` calls can both pass the pre-check and double-charge without a row lock.
findings.why_it_matters: Parallel retries can introduce duplicate side effects under load.
findings.recommended_action: Use transactional idempotency key or SELECT FOR UPDATE before side effects; add test with two concurrent tasks.

findings.observation: Only `test_process_success` exists; no failure or concurrency coverage.
findings.why_it_matters: Tests must fail when retry or race regressions are introduced.
findings.recommended_action: Add tests: transient error then success, max attempts exceeded, concurrent duplicate process.

findings.observation: Body describes backoff timings but not idempotency or worker pool constraints.
findings.why_it_matters: Future readers cannot judge whether duplicate charge risk was accepted.
findings.recommended_action: Add a 'Risks / idempotency' section listing required keys and DB locking approach.

findings.observation: Structured logging refactor in the same file as retry logic.
findings.why_it_matters: Mixed concerns slow review and increase merge conflict risk (small-cls.md).
findings.recommended_action: Move logging refactor to a separate CL or revert it from this change.

findings.observation: README still documents immediate-fail behavior for order processing.
findings.why_it_matters: Operators will misconfigure timeouts when behavior changes.
findings.recommended_action: Update README 'Order processing' with retry bounds and idempotency requirements.

limitations: Did not execute load test; concurrency finding from static analysis.
suggested_next_steps: Split logging refactor into its own CL.
Extract HTTP and retry collaborators; shrink OrderProcessor.
Add idempotency or locking and concurrent tests.
